mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts signals strengthening residential investment and broader economic momentum, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts lead GDP cycles by 2-3 quarters in historical data
failure_condition: Rank IC of HOUST change against 20-day forward returns below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or direction accuracy below 55% in out-of-sample walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls three-month average increase exceeds 200k, the labor market is strengthening reliably, boosting consumer income and spending expectations, which translates into positive equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2010 recovery persistent payroll gains >200k per month correlated with market gains
failure_condition: Direction accuracy of triggered signals below 52% over any 3-year period, or average excess return of triggered vs non-triggered less than 50 bps.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and industrial production indicates synchronized expansion across consumer demand and manufacturing activity, reinforcing corporate earnings visibility and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Co-movement of demand and supply signals broad-based expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC of joint signal (e.g., product of standardized growth rates) remains below 0.04 or negative over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI year-over-year below 2%), the Fed maintains accommodative policy, reducing discount rates and supporting equity valuations, producing positive forward returns; the mechanism is regime-conditional because high inflation flips it negative.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed explicit 2% target creates regime boundary
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return during low inflation regimes not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or Rank IC below 0.03 in periods with CPI <2%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding output capacity and favorable supply conditions, which typically boost corporate profits and stock prices over the medium term as the economy grows.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO historically leads earnings by 2-3 quarters
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO growth vs 60-day forward returns below 0.02 over 5-year rolling window, or direction accuracy below 50% during recession-free periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate (increase >0.5pp within three months) signals imminent recession risk, prompting negative equity market repricing as consumption and output contracts over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:Sahm rule threshold 0.5pp used for recession detection
failure_condition: Median 5-day forward return after trigger is not significantly below zero, or fewer than 70% of triggered events produce negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero and inflation is low, financial conditions are extremely accommodative, compressing risk premiums and raising equity prices; the joint condition of both being low is the interaction that amplifies the signal, while deviation in either proxy reduces it.
evidence_refs: fact:Zero lower bound period 2009-2015 saw low inflation and low rates
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term (product of inverses) against 20-day returns <0.03 over any 3-year window, or joint condition fails to produce positive mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of above-trend GDP growth (year-over-year >2.5%), robust aggregate demand drives corporate revenue and earnings growth, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; this mechanism reverses in below-trend regimes where weak demand impairs earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:US potential growth estimated around 2% at the time
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return in high-growth regime not above unconditional mean, or Rank IC of GDP growth vs returns below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window.
